content.problemStatement:
If I keep moving, they won't know |
I'll morph to someone else |
What they throw at me's too slow |
I'll morph to someone else |
I'm just a ghost |
I'll morph to someone else |
Defense mechanism mode!

You are given two integer sequences A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ and an integer KKK. You may perform the following operation at most 3⋅N3 \cdot N3⋅N times:


Select a contiguous subsequence of AAA with length KKK. Let's denote this subsequence by AL,AL+1,…,AL+K−1A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_{L+K-1}AL​,AL+1​,…,AL+K−1​.
Create an integer sequence S1,S2,…,SKS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KS1​,S2​,…,SK​ which is isomorphic to the selected subsequence ― for each pair of integers iii and jjj (1≤i,j≤K1 \le i, j \le K1≤i,j≤K), Si=SjS_i = S_jSi​=Sj​ must hold if AL−1+i=AL−1+jA_{L-1+i} = A_{L-1+j}AL−1+i​=AL−1+j​ and Si≠SjS_i \neq S_jSi​=Sj​ must hold if AL−1+i≠AL−1+jA_{L-1+i} \neq A_{L-1+j}AL−1+i​=AL−1+j​. In addition, 1≤Si≤1,0001 \le S_i \le 1,0001≤Si​≤1,000 must hold for each valid iii.
For each valid iii, replace AL−1+iA_{L-1+i}AL−1+i​ by SiS_iSi​.
Is it possible to change the sequence AAA to BBB? If it is possible, find a sequence of operations which morphs AAA to BBB.
constraints: 1≤Ai,Bi≤1,0001 \le A_i, B_i \le 1,0001≤Ai​,Bi​≤1,000 for each valid iii

`Explanation**Example case 1:** The state of the sequence after each operation is [1,2,3,4]→[4,3,2,4]→[4,3,2,1][1, 2, 3, 4] \rightarrow [4, 3, 2, 4] \rightarrow [4, 3, 2, 1][1,2,3,4]→[4,3,2,4]→[4,3,2,1].
See the following animation for more clarity:
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to morph AAA to BBB using no more than 3⋅N3 \cdot N3⋅N operations, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, start by printing a line containing the string `"YES"` (without quotes).
Then, print a line containing a single integer PPP (0≤P≤3⋅N0 \le P \le 3 \cdot N0≤P≤3⋅N) ― the number of operations you want to perform.
Finally, print PPP lines. Each of these lines should contain K+1K+1K+1 space-separated integers L,S1,S2,…,SKL, S_1, S_2, \ldots, S_KL,S1​,S2​,…,SK​ describing an operation (1≤L≤N−K+11 \le L \le N-K+11≤L≤N−K+1; 1≤Si≤1,0001 \le S_i \le 1,0001≤Si​≤1,000 for each valid iii).
If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
